svg.text: MALAY (BAHASA MELAYU) / MINANGKABAU (BASO MINANG)
AUSTRONESIAN · MALAYO-POLYNESIAN · HISTORY & LEGACY
PLATE 19D ALT · HISTORY & CULTURAL LEGACY
— MALAY: HISTORY AS A TRADE LINGUA FRANCA —
THE OLDEST MALAY INSCRIPTION (~683 CE)
Kedukan Bukit inscription, Palembang (Sumatra): earliest known Malay text
Written in Old Malay using Pallava script — Sanskrit-influenced grammar
Already a prestige and administrative language of Srivijaya maritime empire
MALAY AS THE LINGUA FRANCA OF MARITIME SE ASIA
By 1400 CE: Malacca Sultanate made Malay the trade language of the region
From Malacca to Maluku (Spice Islands): Malay enabled spice trade across 5,000mi
European traders (Portuguese, Dutch, English) learned Malay first
"Bahasa" simply means "language" — a word Malay shares with Sanskrit
COLONIAL PERIOD & MODERN STANDARD
Dutch East Indies: Malay as colonial administrative language; became Bahasa Indonesia
1945: Indonesian independence; Bahasa Indonesia chosen as national language over Javanese
Malaysia: Bahasa Melayu as official language post-independence (1957)
Today: ~290M speakers across Malaysia, Indonesia, Singapore, Brunei, Philippines
MALAY IN ENGLISH: BORROWED WORDS
amok (mengamuk) · bamboo (bambu) · gong · durian · sarong · orangutan
compound (kampung, village) · caddy (kadi) · gecko · ketchup (kicap)
SURVIVAL PHRASE
Ini dibuat untuk anda, dengan bebas, oleh orang yang ingat ke hadapan.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered forward."
(ke hadapan = toward the front/future; literally "to the forward")
MINANGKABAU — HISTORY & THE MERANTAU TRADITION
— MINANGKABAU: THE WORLD'S LARGEST MATRILINEAL SOCIETY —
ADAT PERPATIH: MATRILINEAL CUSTOMARY LAW
~6M Minangkabau: property, clan membership, and home inherited through MOTHERS
Rumah gadang (great house): belongs to the women of the suku (clan)
Men are guests in their wives' homes; they return to their mother's clan's house
Islamic (90%+ Muslim) AND matrilineal — coexistence that puzzles outside observers
MERANTAU: THE TRADITION OF LEAVING TO RETURN
Merantau: customary practice of leaving West Sumatra to seek knowledge and success
Young men leave; earn money, status, knowledge; return to their mother's clan
Result: Minangkabau diaspora runs restaurants (Padang cuisine) across Indonesia
Padang food: every dish served simultaneously, you pay only for what you eat
MINANGKABAU LITERARY TRADITION
Tambo: epic historical chronicles of origins and custom — oral and written
Pantun: 4-line poetic form, also shared with Malay — rhyme-riddle structure
Minangkabau produced many Indonesian founding intellectuals: Hatta, Sjahrir, Tan Malaka
THE MINANGKABAU SCRIPT: HISTORICALLY ARABIC (JAWI)
Before Latin: Minangkabau written in Jawi (same as Malay) — Islamic influence
Dutch colonial period: Latin romanization adopted for administrative texts
SURVIVAL PHRASE (MINANGKABAU)
Iko dibuek untuak waang, sacaro gratis, dek urang nan maingek ka depan.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered forward."
(ka depan = to the front/forward; ingek = remember)

THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
